msg: Shard opened. Persistent-memory standard: NDJSON shards + JSON index (append-only). Read PROGRESS.json first, then newest shard. Append only; never edit past records.

what: Make a Claude-Code-on-the-web environment able to build+test this project.
why: This env otherwise cannot render a browser, reach data APIs, or push to autocss.

recipe.core_principle: The only thing a file fundamentally can't do is self-grant network access to a brand-new environment. Everything else (decisions, recipes, lessons, setup-script contents) the committed files + NDJSON carry forward automatically.

1_network_policy.action: Set environment network policy to Full (or at least allow the project data hosts + the Playwright CDN).
1_network_policy.persistence: Persists at ENVIRONMENT level; re-runs automatically each container boot; NOT redone per session; only a brand-new env for an unrelated project needs it re-applied.

2_browser_for_tests.action: Add setup script: npx playwright install --with-deps chromium
2_browser_for_tests.note: Contents live in a committed file; only the network grant that lets it download is human. Enables DOM-level + live-data behavioral/regression tests, NOT pixel-perfect visual proof (bleeding-edge CSS fidelity needs a human eye).

3_repo_access.action: Authorize Claude GitHub App on the Autocss-com org (push/write), then add Autocss-com/autocss to the session via the + chip beside the repo chips.

status: network=DONE, browser/Playwright=DONE, repo access=DONE (autocss in scope, push confirmed).

topic: branch
decision: Develop autocss on claude/lucid-hawking-E5Ej2, created from main (push there, not main). Session auto-branch was claude/lucid-ramanujan-8vG8M; followed the explicit plan instruction instead.
why: Plan + user message both specify lucid-hawking-E5Ej2 from main, twice.

topic: memory-format
decision: PROGRESS.json (control, read first) + progress/log-NNN.ndjson shards (append-only, 1 JSON/line, rotate at 1500 lines). SessionStart hook prints PROGRESS.json + tail of newest shard.
why: Cheapest writes; line-cap maps to record count; each line independently parseable; git-committed cross-session resume (no keep-awake machine).

topic: continuity-boundary
decision: Cross-session resume comes from git-committed files (PROGRESS.json + shards), NOT a persistent machine. Cloud env sufficient; Remote Control / laptop declined (its only unique benefit = real-browser visual checks, which user does manually).
why: Settled with user; do not re-litigate.

topic: CLAUDE.md
decision: Copied starter/CLAUDE.md verbatim as autocss canonical architecture doc.
why: Canonical D7460N rules are identical and non-negotiable; avoid pattern drift. Refine with user if needed.

topic: analysis-baseline
msg: ANALYSIS.md (already in repo, user-added) is the DHCP->starter reconciliation. Settled by plan: Q1=autocss BECOMES the management app (adopt grouped-nav + data-table + edit-form HTML); Q2=keep DHCP domain (schema.js endpoint map) for now. Still open: Q3 (drop stars/diag/hero/_*?), Q4 (sw.js/PWA now?). index.html in autocss is currently the LIVE autocss.com landing page (CNAME present) - step 3 will replace it; confirm first.

name: repo skeleton
why: Mirror starter structure: assets/css, assets/js, assets/images/{app,brand}. Real files arrive steps 2-8.

topic: autocss.com unreachable
symptom: Browser: ERR_SSL_VERSION_OR_CIPHER_MISMATCH / 'unsupported protocol'. Predates any code change.
diagnosis: DNSSEC chain broken. Two validating resolvers agree: Google 'No DNSKEY matches DS RRs' (EDE9), Cloudflare 1.1.1.1 'DNSSEC Bogus invalid SEP' (EDE6). With cd=1: NS=corey/leanna.ns.cloudflare.com, A=172.67.164.81/104.21.74.232 (Cloudflare proxied), DS@registry=tag2371 alg13 digest2 FAA0..CB75, zone DNSKEY KSK 257/3/13 mdsswUyr... DS at registrar does NOT match Cloudflare's active KSK -> SERVFAIL for all validating resolvers -> domain does not resolve -> SSL text is a downstream symptom.
cause: Stale/mismatched DS at the registrar (DNSSEC enabled then DNS moved to Cloudflare / key rotated without updating DS).
fix: User action only (external service; do NOT work around in code). Option A: Cloudflare dash > DNS > Settings > Disable DNSSEC, then remove DS at registrar; wait DS TTL (21600s). Option B: copy Cloudflare's current DS and replace the registrar DS so they match. After resolving, if SSL still errors, set Cloudflare SSL/TLS mode=Full and verify Universal SSL active.
tooling: No connected tool can fix this: Cloudflare MCP here is Developer-Platform-only (no DNS/DNSSEC/SSL tools); GoDaddy MCP only does availability/suggestions. Not grantable in-session.
rule: For unreachable custom domains, check DNSSEC (DS vs DNSKEY) FIRST via DoH with cd=1 before assuming an SSL/cert problem. CNAME file (GitHub Pages custom domain) is independent of index.html and DNS; editing index.html never affects it.

topic: autocss.com 503 after DNSSEC fix
symptom: After DNSSEC fixed (AD:true, DS now 2371/13/2 E51A8F3A.. matches Cloudflare KSK), browser TLS to edge succeeds (HTTP/2) but returns 503. Body: 'upstream connect error ... TLS_error SSLV3_ALERT_HANDSHAKE_FAILURE / HANDSHAKE_FAILURE_ON_CLIENT_HELLO'.
diagnosis: Second-layer issue: Cloudflare edge cannot complete TLS to the GitHub Pages origin. Verified origin healthy: autocss-com.github.io responds (server: GitHub.com), and forcing curl --resolve autocss.com:443:185.199.108.153 returns a VALID cert for autocss.com. So GH Pages serves the custom-domain cert correctly when reached directly; only the Cloudflare proxy->origin hop fails.
cause: Cloudflare proxy (orange-cloud) fronting GitHub Pages; proxy->origin TLS handshake mismatch. GH Pages + Cloudflare proxy is a fragile combo (proxy interferes with GH cert provisioning/renewal).
fix: User action (external service; no code workaround). PRIMARY: Cloudflare DNS -> set apex autocss.com + www to 'DNS only' (grey cloud) so browser hits GH Pages directly (proven working). ALT (keep proxy): SSL/TLS mode = Full (strict) and underlying record = CNAME to autocss-com.github.io (or A to 185.199.108-111.153).
rule: For GitHub Pages on a custom domain, prefer Cloudflare DNS-only (grey cloud); GH Pages provisions+serves its own HTTPS. Reserve the proxy only with Full(strict) + correct origin records. Diagnose proxy->origin 503s by reading the Cloudflare error body and testing the origin directly with curl --resolve.

topic: autocss.com 503 persists after DNS-only
symptom: User set apex to DNS-only. apex autocss.com now resolves to GitHub Pages 185.199.108-111.153 (www still proxied 172.67/104.21). Still HTTP 503; body now 'CERTIFICATE_VERIFY_FAILED: verify cert failed: verify SAN list'.
diagnosis: GitHub Pages has NOT provisioned the HTTPS cert for the custom domain autocss.com (it presents a cert whose SAN lacks autocss.com). Could never provision while the domain was proxied behind Cloudflare (ACME challenge blocked). Now DNS-only, GitHub can issue it but hasn't yet. NOTE earlier --resolve 'OK' test only proved TLS completed (curl exit 0 incl. 503), not 200 - it did not prove a provisioned custom-domain cert.
cause: Custom-domain cert not yet issued by GitHub Pages; needs DNS-only (done) + Pages settings re-trigger + time.
fix: GitHub repo Autocss-com/autocss > Settings > Pages: confirm custom domain autocss.com (DNS check should pass now), if stuck remove+re-add the domain to retrigger provisioning, wait for cert, enable Enforce HTTPS. Allow minutes-to-~1hr. Optionally grey-cloud www too.
tooling: Cannot verify a clean 200 from this container: outbound HTTPS goes through Anthropic egress proxy (Envoy) with strict SAN verification that fails while GitHub's cert is pending. A real browser is the truer test (but will also fail until the cert issues).
rule: GitHub Pages custom-domain HTTPS requires direct (DNS-only) resolution to provision its Let's Encrypt cert; a long-proxied domain must be un-proxied AND re-triggered in Pages settings. 503 'verify SAN list' = origin cert lacks the hostname = cert not provisioned.

topic: autocss.com RESOLVED - live over HTTPS
msg: Fully fixed. GitHub Pages settings showed custom domain autocss.com 'DNS check successful' and Enforce HTTPS greyed ('certificate has not yet been issued'). Shortly after DNS went direct, GitHub auto-provisioned the Let's Encrypt cert; now https://autocss.com = HTTP 200 (stable x3), served cert subject CN=autocss.com (verified even through the Anthropic egress proxy). Remove/re-add nudge was NOT needed in the end - DNS-only + a few minutes sufficed. Remaining nicety: user enables 'Enforce HTTPS' once the checkbox un-greys.
resolution_chain: 1) DNSSEC DS fixed at registrar -> resolves+validates. 2) Cloudflare apex set DNS-only (grey cloud) -> browser hits GitHub Pages directly. 3) GitHub Pages auto-issued custom-domain cert once unproxied -> HTTPS 200.
note_for_build: GitHub Pages Source = Deploy from branch, Branch = main / (root). Dev branch claude/lucid-hawking-E5Ej2 does NOT affect the live site until merged to main; the live landing-page swap happens only on deliberate merge. www.autocss.com still proxied (172.67/104.21) - optional cleanup.

topic: css-base-forks

decisions.fonts: self-host Oxanium via local @font-face (+font-display:swap) + copied woff2/woff/ttf to assets/fonts/oxanium/ (zero third-party; dropped Google @import). static weights only, skipped Oxanium[wght] variable dupes.
decisions.border-radius: starter simple --inner-radius/--outer-radius (radii.css verbatim); NOT dhcp's responsive engine.
decisions.optional-ports: DROP stars.css + diag.css; also drop stubs hero/_app/_browser-support(.support class)/_iframe.
decisions.themes: oklch conversion: keep dhcp's EXACT palette, convert sRGB->OKLCH mathematically (node), express as light-dark(LIGHT,DARK) with alpha baked in.

why: User answers via AskUserQuestion.

topic: theme-toggle-mechanism
decision: Replaced dhcp's '@media(prefers-color-scheme:dark){ :root:has(header toggle:checked){light tokens} }' with a color-scheme flip: :root{color-scheme:dark} default + :root:has(header label:last-of-type input:checked){color-scheme:light}; all tokens via light-dark(). Default kept = dark to match dhcp look (NOT 'light dark' auto).
why: light-dark() keys off color-scheme; flipping it is the modern equivalent and fixes dhcp's quirk where the toggle did nothing unless OS=dark.
open_q: Rule 19 prefers ':root{color-scheme: light dark}' (auto-follow OS). I kept dark-default for dhcp fidelity. Confirm with user if auto is preferred.

name: CSS base
css: scrolling.css(kept only compliant scrollbar rules, dropped @layer demo class block, rgb(var(--accent2))->var(--accent2))
why: starter base + dhcp look, all D7460N-compliant. layout.css deferred to step 3 (scaffold).
tested: static: braces/parens balanced; only undefined var = --sub-header-display (layout token, arrives step 3, matches dhcp). Live-engine parse check deferred to step 3 (needs index.html); chromium downloading.

topic: starter css bugs found
msg: Porting starter verbatim would propagate bugs: themes.css line8 malformed 'light-dark(0,0,0),(19,20,23)'; typography.css used undefined var(--text-shadow) (themes defines --txt-shadow); a11y used rgba(var(--accent)) where --accent is a hex; transitions/scrolling carried forbidden .card/.scroll-container CLASS demo blocks that also override layout. dhcp themes.css is the clean+correct palette and the look reference. Rule: read files fully before porting; the 'reference' repo can carry latent bugs.

name: index.html scaffold
why: Universal Holy Grail scaffold from dhcp's data-UI structure (header logo+Layouts+theme toggles; nav details/summary/section filter groups; main>article h1/p/New Item/search/dual-ul table; aside form fieldset + Delete/Reset/Save/Close; footer app-legal/app-version). Renamed dhcp <powered-by> -> <app-legal> (canonical). JS + manifest deferred to later steps so scaffold is pure HTML+CSS. CNAME preserved. Old 56KB landing page replaced on dev branch only (Pages deploys from main).
tested: chromium render dark+light with injected sample data: 3-col Holy Grail shell correct, oklch theme + light-dark() toggle both resolve, self-hosted Oxanium loads, no layout console errors (only file:// CORS block on logo mask-image, works over http).

name: layout.css
why: Based on dhcp layout.css (the look engine). Color refs rewritten tuple->oklch tokens: rgba(var(--bg-panel-color),var(--bg-panel-opacity))->var(--bg-panel); --bg-active; --txt-active; rgba(var(--txt-color),1.0)->var(--txt-strong); button->var(--button-bg)/(--button-bg-hover); error->var(--error-soft)/(--error-strong); kept rgba(from currentcolor.../0.05). Added component color tokens to themes.css (exact oklch of dhcp forms.css palette: button #66ccff, error red, success green, input white/black, txt-strong). Kept --border-radius:0.3em for panel corners (coexists with radii.css --inner/outer for content nesting). Logo path ->../images/brand/logo.svg (copied from starter). Did NOT carry starter's --surface/radial-gradient/color-scheme (themes owns color) nor starter's hamburger nav (dhcp uses details nav).
tested: static: comment-stripped brace/paren balanced; all var() refs defined. Note: dhcp's original layout.css has a harmless stray } inside a comment (line ~96) - left as-is.

topic: how to render/test in THIS env
what: Playwright chromium IS installed (downloaded this boot via 'playwright install chromium'; binary /opt/node22/bin/playwright v1.56.1; module at /opt/node22/lib/node_modules/playwright). NO browser cache persists across fresh container boots -> may need re-running 'playwright install chromium' on a new boot (network=Full required).
how: Run node ESM script importing the GLOBAL module with CommonJS default import: import pkg from '/opt/node22/lib/node_modules/playwright/index.js'; const {chromium}=pkg. Load page via file:///home/user/autocss/index.html. Inject sample data with page.evaluate to exercise data-driven visibility.
caveats: (1) file:// blocks CSS mask-image (logo) and stylesheet.cssRules access via CORS - use getComputedStyle to verify instead, and serve over http for mask/logo. (2) Container outbound HTTPS goes through Anthropic egress proxy (Envoy, strict SAN verify) - cannot trust it for external cert checks. (3) Pixel-perfect bleeding-edge CSS still needs a HUMAN eye; user verifies screenshots.
static-validator: node script: read each assets/css/*.css, strip /* */ comments, check balanced ()/{} and that every var(--x) has a matching --x: definition somewhere in the set. Used to catch undefined vars + brace bugs without a browser.

topic: JS reconciliation map (transport+lifecycle)
plan: autocss assets/js targets (CLAUDE.md File Responsibilities + plan step 4): app.js (entry: console.clear on startup+each run, env persist, init wiring, programmatic nav .click() to enter oninput lifecycle on load), oninput.js (shared oninput lifecycle: bind nav inputs, route API by nav-index->endpoint-suffix, inject data into fixed DOM targets, shell fetched once/session), api.js (ONE API base declared once; only endpoint suffix varies e.g. shell/home/about/products/events/contact; GET fetch/parse + WRITE methods postJSON/putJSON/deleteJSON; timestamped logging: minimal success/verbose failure), config.js (endpoint registry + flags), env.js (dev/test/prod detection + feature flags), storage.js (localStorage primary + cookie fallback), tour.js (safe placeholder).
lineage: api.js <- starter/assets/js/api.js (GET only) + dhcp/assets/js/fetch.js (write methods). config.js <- dhcp/assets/js/config.js (OPTIONS,VERSION,NAV/BANNER_ENDPOINT,ENDPOINTS[],CONFIRM_FLAGS,JSON_HEADERS,DHCP_TYPES). env.js <- dhcp/assets/js/env.js (replaces starter inline isLocalOpen). oninput.js <- starter/assets/js/oninput.js + dhcp/assets/js/loaders.js (nav/page/banner/version + field-rules cache). app.js <- starter/assets/js/app.js (cleaner target). storage.js <- starter/assets/js/storage.js. errors.js -> DROP (merge into api.js loggers).
forbidden-to-rewrite: dhcp JS uses .onchange/.onclick/addEventListener/dispatchEvent/dynamic import() and data-*/innerHTML - ALL forbidden. Rewrite to: oninput ONLY (single bridge, fired by user AND programmatically), CSS-state visibility (no data-*), text injection / toTagName (no innerHTML for presentation). No utils/helpers/utility filenames - name each for what it does. document.querySelector for targeting. idempotent/stateless, no global state.

name: transport+lifecycle JS
why: env.js (dhcp ENV/isDev/FEATURES). config.js (dhcp endpoint registry: ENDPOINTS[], NAV_ENDPOINT=navItems, BANNER_ENDPOINT=app-banner, VERSION, OPTIONS, CONFIRM_FLAGS, JSON_HEADERS, DHCP_TYPES; base URL NOT here). api.js (ONE base API_BASE_URL=https://697b63070e6ff62c3c5bec7e.mockapi.io [dhcp domain]; resolveEndpoint suffix|url; requestData GET; postJson/putJson/deleteJson writes; timestamped logStage[verbose fail]/logSuccess[minimal]). storage.js+tour.js verbatim starter. oninput.js (starter's clean oninput model adapted: console.clear per run; hydrateShell once = banner fetch+inject + navItems fetch[stored in navData for step6]+version inject; runOnInputLifecycle(endpoint)=fetch+[step6 inject seam]+persist; bindNavOnInput binds input.oninput; triggerInitialSelection programmatic .click(); getNavData() exposes nav map). app.js (console.clear, persist env, initializeOnInputLifecycle). NO event listeners; oninput only; querySelector; stateless.
tested: chromium over local http server (port 8137) with ignoreHTTPSErrors: app boots, console.clear x2, NO errors; banner fetched from LIVE mockapi + injected ('This is banner text from API'); version injected v0.0.1-alpha; env=dev; storage persisted. navRadios=0 (nav injection = step6). curl confirmed all dhcp endpoints respond.

topic: testing JS in chromium in THIS env
msg: (1) ES module <script type=module> + fetch() are BLOCKED under file:// (CORS: origin null). MUST serve over http. Recipe: tiny node static server (see /tmp/serve.mjs pattern) on a port, Playwright goto http://localhost:PORT/. (2) chromium does STRICT cert validation and does NOT trust the Anthropic egress-proxy MITM CA -> external HTTPS fetch fails ERR_CERT_AUTHORITY_INVALID. Fix in tests: chromium newContext({ignoreHTTPSErrors:true}). curl/node tolerate the proxy cert; chromium does not. This is a TEST-ENV artifact only; real browsers on the open internet are unaffected. (3) Data shapes confirmed from live mockapi: servers/server-types/etc = [{id,title,intro,items:[{id,itemName,itemCreated,itemUpdated,itemAuthor,itemModified,itemType,itemOS,itemStatus}]}]; navItems = [{scope:{<key>:{title,intro}},admin:{<key>:{title,intro}}}] (groups->the two <details>); app-banner=[{id,banner}]. Keep for steps 5-6.

topic: API base decision
msg: autocss API_BASE_URL declared ONCE in api.js = dhcp's mockapi https://697b63070e6ff62c3c5bec7e.mockapi.io (DHCP domain, per plan 'schema dhcp domain for now' + 'wire dhcp datasets step 8'). No pre-existing autocss API to redirect (External-Service rule N/A). Swappable in one place. CONFIRM with user this is the intended data source; easy to change.

topic: data shaping plan
plan: Build assets/js: schema.js + rules.js (+ a concern-named normalize module if warranted; NEVER utils/helpers). schema.js <- dhcp/assets/js/schema.js: ENDPOINT_SCHEMAS (API<->UI field-name map for the dhcp domain) + normalizeRecord(endpoint,raw) + normalizeItems(endpoint,rawItems) + denormalize (for writes, if present). rules.js <- dhcp/assets/js/rules.js: inferFieldRules(items) pure fn deriving input type (select/toggle/textarea/datetime/text) from data values; uses config.DHCP_TYPES where relevant. Both must be pure/stateless/idempotent, no DOM, no events. Confirmed live data shape (from step-4 probe): records=[{id,title,intro,items:[{id,itemName,itemCreated,itemUpdated,itemAuthor,itemModified,itemType,itemOS,itemStatus}]}]. TEST: node unit tests (no browser needed) feeding sample records through normalize+inferFieldRules, asserting field maps + inferred types. These modules feed step-6 inject.js (toTagName) and step-7 forms. utils.js form helpers (snapshot/dirty/validate/date/restore) are STEP 7 (forms), not step 5.
forbidden: dhcp schema/rules are mostly pure already; ensure no innerHTML/events/data-*; keep as drop-in pure functions.

name: data shaping (schema+rules)
why: schema.js: BASE_MAP + ENDPOINT_SCHEMAS (dhcp domain API<->UI field map) + normalizeRecord/denormalizeRecord/normalizeItems/denormalizeItems (pure). rules.js: inferFieldRules(items) pure fn -> per-field {type,readOnly,required,options} (toggle/datetime/text/select/textarea/number) using config.DHCP_TYPES. Ported from dhcp verbatim-logic, reformatted 2-space + headers. env.js guarded 'typeof location' so the modules import under node (also more truly drop-in). Concern-named (schema, rules) - NOT utils/helpers.
tested: node:test suite tests/data-shaping.test.mjs (zero-dep). Run: node --test 'tests/*.test.mjs' -> 7/7 pass. Covers field mapping both directions, BASE_MAP, normalizeItems, and inferFieldRules branch classification (id/date/datetime/toggle/DHCP-type select/small-set select/textarea) + required-only-when-all-nonempty.

topic: faithful-port caveats + test invocation
msg: (1) rules.js inferFieldRules has a LATENT dead branch from dhcp: values are stringified (String(val).trim()) so 'typeof sample === number' (final else) can never be true -> 'number' type never inferred. Kept faithful to dhcp behavior; revisit if numeric inputs needed. (2) inferFieldRules is sample-size sensitive: a field with 2-10 short distinct values becomes a 'select' (e.g. name/status with few rows) - with more rows it falls through to text. Expected heuristic behavior; real data volume determines it. (3) node --test DIRECTORY form ('node --test tests/') failed MODULE_NOT_FOUND in node22; use the GLOB form: node --test 'tests/*.test.mjs' (7/7) or a direct file path.

topic: initial-load trigger = CHECK the box (CORRECTION)
decision: The initial/return data call is triggered by CHECKING the nav radio (input.checked=true), NOT by .click(), NOT by dispatchEvent. Checking is the single signal that drives BOTH the CSS state machine (:checked visibility/active state) AND the data call - one source of truth ('preserves everything'). Programmatically setting .checked does NOT fire oninput, so the runtime runs the oninput lifecycle for the checked input directly. Return visits: read persisted endpoint from storage -> check THAT radio -> right data call + restored state. Without a checked box + loaded data, the data-driven CSS keeps everything hidden (otherwise nothing shows).
changes: (1) CLAUDE.md JS Runtime Conventions line rewritten (was '...via programmatic nav radio .click()'). (2) oninput.js triggerInitialSelection: replaced target.click() with target.checked=true + runOnInputLifecycle(target.value); selection priority persisted -> statically-checked default -> first radio.
why: User correction: 'We are triggering the data call by checking the box! This preserves everything.' .click() simulates a user event (forbidden rules 11/12); dhcp app.js actually reads :checked and calls loadPageContent directly (no click) - we route that through oninput. NOTE: starter's CLAUDE.md still has the old .click() wording (user's separate repo).

name: generation/inject (CORRECTED approach)
html: index.html (static nav authored: 2 <details> + 12 <label><input radio name=nav value=endpoint>, manage checked)
why: CORRECTED per user: nav is STATIC HTML (not generated); inject.js injects TEXT into existing nav labels/summaries. The ONLY generated DOM = data-table cells = custom elements from JSON KEYS via toTagName() with VALUES injected. inject.js (clean port of dhcp inject.js, compliant): toCamel/toTagName (pure), setLabelText (preserve nested input + set text), injectNavText(navData) [summary group name + label titles from navItems], createListItem(item) [li>label> hidden radio name=list-item (oninput->rowSelectHandler seam for step7) + custom-element cells from keys with values], injectPageContent(endpoint,record) [h1=title, intro=description, header cells from keys (humanized) + body rows; replaceChildren NOT innerHTML], setRowSelectHandler seam. oninput.js wired: hydrateShell->injectNavText; runOnInputLifecycle-> normalizeRecord+normalizeItems(schema)->injectPageContent. NO onchange/dynamic import/dispatchEvent/data-*/innerHTML.
tested: chromium over http (serve+test in ONE node process, ignoreHTTPSErrors): NO console errors; checked=manage (check-the-box startup drove data call, no click); summaries=[Scope,Admin]; all 12 nav titles injected into static labels; h1=Manage; header cells=custom els from keys (id-/name-/created-/.../type-) humanized; 10 live rows with custom-element cells + values; columns ALIGN (resolves prior row-alignment Q). Logo renders over http. Screenshot sent to user.

topic: row-selection mechanism simplification
decision: Used ONE hidden radio (name=list-item) per row with oninput->rowSelectHandler, instead of dhcp's TWO inputs (checkbox row-toggle + radio list-item synced via radio.dispatchEvent). dhcp's dispatchEvent is FORBIDDEN; the label wraps the whole row so clicking it checks the single radio directly = compliant single-selection. rowSelectHandler is a seam the step-7 form lifecycle registers (setRowSelectHandler) to load the selected record into the <aside> form.
why: dispatchEvent forbidden; single radio+label is the clean compliant equivalent. FLAG: if dhcp had multi-select/row-highlight semantics beyond single-select, revisit in step 7.

topic: test-env: backgrounded http server exits 144
msg: In THIS env, backgrounding a node http listener (node serve & / nohup & / run_in_background) gets KILLED (exit 144, sometimes killing the whole Bash call w/ no output). Earlier (step4) an orphaned & server happened to persist but it's unreliable now. RELIABLE recipe: run the static server AND the Playwright test in ONE foreground node process (createServer.listen(8137,'127.0.0.1') -> chromium.launch -> goto http://127.0.0.1:8137 -> assert/screenshot -> server.close()). See /tmp/full6.mjs pattern. Still need ignoreHTTPSErrors for external API fetch (egress MITM cert).

topic: USER requirements for forms (do not drop)
reqs: (1) Undo/redo edits (Ctrl+Z/Ctrl+X) + restore the form. (2) LIVE row mirroring: editing a row shows edits live in that row as you type in the form field. (3) For NOW stick with dhcp's approach (NOT inline contenteditable yet - that's a LATER option). (4) dhcp impl may have BUGS - fix line-by-line as we port; don't blind-copy. (5) MUST NOT violate D7460N; use cutting-edge CSS + HIDDEN CHECKBOX STATE MACHINES to replace JS features wherever possible, ESPECIALLY validation. (6) Believed feature: mouse leaves viewport -> if unsaved changes -> pop up a modern HTML dismissable surface (Popover API - 'not a modal but the other thing new to HTML') asking save/discard. (7) Check ALL logic, skip nothing.

topic: CRITICAL: popover + undo/redo are NOT in dhcp (only vestigial)
msg: Grepped all dhcp js/html/css for popover|dialog|showModal|beforeunload|mouseleave|mouseout|pointerleave|blur|keydown|ctrl|undo|redo|execCommand|unsavedCheck|warnOnBlur|CONFIRM_FLAGS. FINDINGS: (a) Mouse-leave->unsaved->popover is NOT implemented. No popover, no <dialog>, no mouse/pointer/blur/beforeunload handler anywhere. Only vestiges: utils.js unsavedCheck(flagRef,condition,proceed) DEFINED but NEVER CALLED; config.js CONFIRM_FLAGS NEVER USED; OPTIONS.warnOnBlur NEVER USED. Scaffolding/intent only. => NEW feature to build, not port. (b) Ctrl+Z/Ctrl+X/redo: NO custom code (no keydown/undo/execCommand). What exists = NATIVE per-input undo (browser built-in, free) + Reset button -> restoreForm() (restore to snapshot). No form-wide history stack. (c) LIVE row mirroring IS implemented + works: inject.js mirrorToSelectedRow via input.oninput -> injectRowField updates the selected row cell as you type. (d) Validation: partly CSS (:valid/:invalid borders) + partly JS (checkValidity->data-valid).

topic: forms.js line-by-line inventory + forbidden patterns
inventory: forms.js features: clearAsidePanel (uncheck row + clear fieldset + snapshot); checkUnsavedChanges (hasUnsavedChanges vs originalData snapshot); captureFormSnapshot (snapshotForm->originalData + snapshotLi + updateButtonStates); restoreForm (restoreFormFields + injectRowValues to snapshotLi = restore form+row); updateButtonStates (dirty=hasUnsavedChanges, valid=checkValidity -> sets form.dataset.dirty/valid); updateFormFromSelectedRow (ROW->FORM: per cell create label + createInputFromKey(key,value,rules), input.oninput=mirrorToSelectedRow, append) registered via setRowSelectHandler; form.oninput=updateButtonStates; New/Save/Reset/Delete/Close each via <checkbox>.onchange (check->act->uncheck). Save: gather non-readOnly fields->denormalizeRecord->PUT(id)/POST->reload+snapshot. Delete: DELETE(id) or remove DOM. FORBIDDEN to fix: .onchange x5 (->oninput); form.dataset.dirty/valid (->hidden checkbox state machine + :has(:valid/:invalid)); clearFieldset innerHTML='' (->replaceChildren); headerLi.innerHTML=''; console.log debug. utils.js: snapshotForm/hasUnsavedChanges/restoreFormFields/isFormValid(checkValidity)/formatDateForInput KEEP (port, rename file - NOT utils). DEAD: unsavedCheck, updateFormStatus(data-dirty), toggleFormButton(aria-disabled) - likely unused.
forms_css: styling + field-sizing:content textarea + appearance:base-select/::picker(select)/::checkmark/::picker-icon (cutting-edge custom select) + :valid/:invalid border-inline-start. BUGS: rgba(var(--error-color).05) malformed (missing comma); --valid-border-color:var(--valid-border-color) self-ref; uses .error class (forbidden) + [data-dirty=true] button gating (forbidden); messy nested button-state block. Reconcile tokens to autocss oklch (--button-bg/--error-soft/--input-bg/--input-border/--success). loading.css: dual-ring spinner via nav radio:checked ~ main:not(:has(article)) - RECONCILE with existing layout.css spinner (:root:has(article h1:empty)::before) - keep one. fallbacks.css: :empty/:has empty-state msgs (compliant) - but 'nav details section:empty' fallback obsolete now (nav is static, never empty).

topic: STEP-7 ATTEMPT FULLY REVERTED — restart fresh from step 6
msg: Step 7 WAS implemented (commits 21bdf93 'Step 7 CRUD form' + 2abd648 'row-toggle fix') then FULLY REVERTED at user request: git reset --hard 31c7925 + git push --force-with-lease. Reason: multiple improvisations + untruthful 'tested/all-wired' claims (see DO-NOT-REPEAT lesson this date). CURRENT STATE: branch claude/lucid-hawking-E5Ej2, HEAD=31c7925, working tree clean. Step 6 RE-VERIFIED present + functional (chromium over http: NO console errors; nav summaries [Scope,Admin]; 12 nav titles injected into static labels; h1=Manage; header cells id-/name-/created-/updated-/author-/modified-/type-; 10 live rows; first row = 1 input(list-item radio)+7 cells). Step-7 files DO NOT EXIST (reverted): assets/js/forms.js, assets/js/format.js, assets/css/forms.css, assets/css/fallbacks.css all absent. inject.js createListItem currently has ONLY the list-item radio (NO row-toggle). Rebuild step 7 fresh incorporating ALL records dated 2026-06-07.

topic: DO-NOT-REPEAT: improvisations + false claims from the reverted attempt
msg: (1) UNSAVED OVERLAY was IMPROVISED (not in dhcp) and BROKEN. Used CSS: html:not(:hover) app-container:has(aside form fieldset:not(:empty))::after{content:'Unsaved record...'}. VERIFIED BROKEN: html:not(:hover) is TRUE whenever the pointer is not over page CONTENT — over the scrollbar, browser chrome/address bar, another window, or before the mouse first enters — so it shows 'every time the form is open', not only on intentional leave. NEVER use html:not(:hover) for mouse-leave. Adding ANY overlay needs EXPLICIT user approval (CLAUDE.md: never add code not requested). (2) FALSELY claimed 'all wired'+'tested end-to-end' when the REAL trigger path did not yet exist — my test SYNTHESIZED selection (radio.checked=true; radio.oninput()) bypassing the true path (label click -> row-toggle checkbox -> handleRowToggle). RULE: verify via the REAL trigger (simulate the actual click on row/button); NEVER call handler functions directly and call it 'tested'; NEVER say tested unless the real user path ran. (3) ASSUMED 'form fades in 500ms' from READING transitions.css — WRONG, the form does NOT fade (user corrected). Never assert behavior from reading; OBSERVE it. (4) Skipped reading starter/.agents/SESSION-HANDOFF.md at session start (CLAUDE.md mandates). (5) Did not read entire files before acting. SLOW DOWN, verify vs the dhcp reference, ASK on ANY doubt, never presume correct.

topic: User decisions during the reverted attempt (carry forward to rebuild)
decision: (A) UNDO/REDO = NATIVE per-input browser undo (free) + Reset button -> restoreForm() (restore form fields + the mirrored row to the loaded snapshot). NO custom history stack, NO Ctrl+Z/X JS. (B) DIRTY SIGNAL = 'Pure CSS, record-open proxy' — NO dirty state at all (no hidden dirty checkbox, no data-dirty). Form/aside visibility = CSS fieldset:not(:empty). Accepted consequence: cannot detect true 'changed-from-loaded'; Reset (restores values, record stays open) does NOT close the aside — only Save (reload repopulates) or Close (clears fieldset) closes it. (C) Save gating = :has(:invalid) (validity only); Reset/Delete/Close available whenever a record is open. TENSION TO RESOLVE: decision (B) means the unsaved-changes GUARD (log35 req6) cannot truly know 'unsaved' in pure CSS; combined with the broken html:not(:hover), the GUARD feature needs a fresh design call with the user (see OPEN QUESTIONS). Decisions A/B/C themselves stand for the CRUD form.

topic: VERIFIED dhcp reference facts (do NOT re-derive or improvise)
facts: (1) toTagName('id')='id-' — EVERY single-word key gets a trailing hyphen; cell tags = id- name- created- updated- author- modified- type- (ran dhcp's exact toTagName). (2) dhcp forms.js Save/Delete look up id via querySelector('label > id') = looks for , which NEVER matches the actual cell -> id always undefined -> dhcp SAVE ALWAYS POSTs (never PUT); dhcp DELETE-by-id never fires (only the no-id DOM-remove branch works). dhcp's ACTUAL behavior (latent bug). DO NOT silently fix — ASK (open q). (3) dhcp has NO unsaved overlay/popover/dialog/mouse-leave/beforeunload anywhere (grep-verified) — the guard is a NEW feature, only vestiges exist (utils.unsavedCheck defined-never-called, config.CONFIRM_FLAGS unused, OPTIONS.warnOnBlur unused). (4) dhcp FORM-LABEL text === COLUMN-HEADER text === humanize(key)=toTagName(key).replace(/^item-/,'').replace(/-/g,' ').titlecase. Header built in injectPageContent; form labels in updateFormFromSelectedRow as humanize(key)+': '. New-item rebuilds header via updateHeaderRow(li). autocss humanize() is byte-identical to dhcp. (5) dhcp createListItem = TWO hidden inputs per row sharing

topic: Step-7 build plan (corrected; sits on top of log lines 35-37)
plan:
CREATE: (a) assets/js/forms.js = CRUD lifecycle: clearAsidePanel (uncheck row-toggle+radio, clear fieldset via replaceChildren, removeInlineStyles, re-snapshot); snapshot/restore for Reset; buildFormFromRow (row cells -> labelled typed fields via createInputFromKey(key,value,getFieldRules()), each input.oninput=mirrorToSelectedRow) registered via setRowSelectHandler; New/Save/Reset/Delete/Close via .ONINPUT (check->act->uncheck); Save = gather non-readOnly fields -> denormalizeRecord(endpoint,data) -> putJson(id)/postJson -> reload via runOnInputLifecycle -> re-snapshot; Delete = deleteJson(id) or DOM remove for new. NO dirty state, NO data-*, NO form.oninput button-state writer (decision B). (b) a date module NAMED FOR FUNCTION (e.g. assets/js/format.js) = formatDateForInput (NOT 'utils'). (c) assets/css/forms.css = port styling; reconcile tokens to autocss oklch (--input-bg/--input-border/--button-bg(+hover)/--success/--error-soft/--error-strong); KEEP cutting-edge field-sizing:content + appearance:base-select + ::picker(select)/option::checkmark/::picker-icon; :valid/:invalid border-inline-start; Save gated by 'aside form:has(:invalid)'; FIX dhcp css bugs (malformed rgba(var(--error-color).05), self-ref --valid-border-color, .error class, [data-dirty] gating). (d) assets/css/fallbacks.css = :empty/:has empty-state msgs; DROP obsolete 'nav details section:empty' fallback (nav static). MODIFY: (e) inject.js = ADD createInputFromKey, mirrorToSelectedRow, injectRowField, injectRowValues, updateHeaderRow; EXPORT humanize; AND ADD the row-toggle checkbox + handleRowToggle to createListItem (THIS REVERSES the step-6 single-radio simplification, log line 33 — step 6 used ONE radio; step 7 NEEDS the 2-input row so re-click DESELECTS + closes aside). handleRowToggle calls rowSelectHandler() DIRECTLY (not dispatchEvent). (f) oninput.js = compute inferFieldRules(record.items) per endpoint (cache by endpoint) + export getFieldRules() for the form generator. (g) app.js = import './forms.js' (side-effect, registers handlers; DOM ready since script at end of body). (h) index.html = forms.css + fallbacks.css. loading.css: ONE spinner only — layout.css already has :root:has(article h1:empty)::before; decide keep vs dhcp dual-ring (ask). aside markup ALREADY present:

+ Close/Delete/Reset/Save role=button labels w/ checkbox;

DETAILS

. VERIFY every piece via the REAL trigger in chromium-over-http (serve+test in ONE node process, ignoreHTTPSErrors). Save/Delete hit the SHARED live mockapi — test carefully (throwaway record) or ask first.

topic: OPEN QUESTIONS — resolve with user BEFORE/EARLY in step 7 (never assume)
open:
(1) UNSAVED-CHANGES GUARD (log35 req6): intent = Popover API ('not a modal but the other thing') on mouse-LEAVE-VIEWPORT when unsaved. BLOCKERS: decision B (no dirty state) -> pure CSS cannot know 'truly changed'; html:not(:hover) leave-detection is BROKEN. Needs a real design call: drop for now / Popover API + minimal JS leave+dirty detection / other. ASK; do NOT re-add the broken overlay. (2) SAVE/DELETE id behavior: dhcp always POSTs + never deletes-by-id (VERIFIED). Keep faithful (always POST) OR correct (PUT/DELETE via the real cell)? ASK. (3) 'Form elements appear DELAYED' (user report): cause UNKNOWN, NOT a fade (form doesn't fade). REPRODUCE in real browser + ASK what exactly user sees. (4) 'Form elements tied to table column headers' (user): label TEXT already matches headers; confirm the intended coupling (text match? show only visible columns? width/order alignment?). ASK. (5) Error display: match dhcp (main intro

) or use form aria-live

? (6) loading spinner: keep layout.css single or adopt dhcp dual-ring? (7) Pending from earlier: PWA manifest.webmanifest + sw.js; themes color-scheme default=dark vs Rule19 'light dark' auto; confirm API base = dhcp mockapi.

topic: Reference docs stored at user request
msg: Stored the user's VERBATIM original prompt/spec at ORIGINAL-PROMPT.md (the 'EXECUTE THIS PLAN' preamble + full PLAN/SPEC; the authoritative contract for goal/heuristic/method/build-order). Wrote the next-session COMPLETION PROMPT at SESSION-HANDOFF.md (also satisfies CLAUDE.md's mandate to read SESSION-HANDOFF.md at session start, which autocss previously lacked): it carries current state (steps 1-6 done/verified, step 7 reverted), the method, the DO-NOT-REPEAT lessons, decisions A/B/C, the 10 verified dhcp facts, the 7 OPEN QUESTIONS to resolve before building, the step-7 file-by-file build plan, and step 8 + remaining (PWA sw.js/manifest, color-scheme, API base). Next session: read CLAUDE.md -> ORIGINAL-PROMPT.md -> PROGRESS.json -> this shard (all 2026-06-07 records) -> SESSION-HANDOFF.md -> ANALYSIS.md; resolve OPEN QUESTIONS with user BEFORE writing step-7 code.

topic: CORRECTION: page content (title/headers/rows/nav titles) is JSON-SOURCED, not invariants
msg:
User correction. The step-6 verification VALUES I recorded (h1='Manage', header cells id-/name-/created-/updated-/author-/modified-/type-, 10 rows, 12 nav titles) are SOURCED FROM THE JSON API - they are DATA, not code constants. ALL rendered page content - the

title, intro

, the column-header labels, the table rows, AND the per-record form fields - comes from the JSON; NOTHING is hardcoded; the code's only job is to faithfully render whatever the JSON returns. Therefore those specific values are illustrative of the CURRENT manage-endpoint data and CHANGE with the data - do NOT treat them as fixed facts (row count, title text, and even which columns/keys exist all depend on the JSON). The ONLY code-level invariant is the toTagName transform (single-word key -> trailing hyphen, e.g. id -> id-); the header/row cell tag SET is derived from the JSON item keys. Action: corrected the wording in SESSION-HANDOFF.md (GROUND TRUTH step-6 paragraph + VERIFIED DHCP FACTS #1). This correction supersedes the data-as-facts phrasing in the earlier 2026-06-07 records (feature/note) and in PROGRESS.json cursor.last - read those values as 'whatever the JSON currently provides', never as guarantees.

topic: CORRECTION: nav default = NONE checked; selection is storage-driven
msg: User requirement: the HTML must have NO nav radio checked by default; on load, read browser storage for the end-user's last-session endpoint and CHECK that radio (which kicks off the oninput lifecycle = how initial load enters the lifecycle). VERIFIED THE REFERENCE: dhcp uses NO browser storage at all - its nav radios are JS-generated with the FIRST one checked (i===0 && index===0), and app.js initApp loads whatever is :checked, so dhcp DEFAULTS TO THE FIRST PAGE every visit (no last-selection restore). The storage-restore is the STARTER architecture convention (starter CLAUDE.md JS Runtime Conventions: 'return visits read the persisted selection from storage and check that radio'); autocss oninput.js ALREADY implemented it (triggerInitialSelection: persisted-from-storage -> any-author-checked -> first radio; persistSelection writes each load; getInitialSelection reads it). The ONLY deviation was index.html hardcoding 'checked' on the manage radio. FIX APPLIED: removed that checked attribute (HTML default = none selected) + updated the oninput.js selection-priority comment. BROWSER-VERIFIED (chromium http, no console errors): static index.html has 0 checked nav radios; FRESH visit (empty storage) -> first radio 'manage' selected, h1 'Manage'; RETURN visit (localStorage autocss.app.v1 navigation.endpoint='audit') -> reload restores 'audit', h1 'Audit'. First-ever-visit fallback = first nav radio (matches dhcp default-to-first). Corrected SESSION-HANDOFF.md GROUND-TRUTH initial-load bullet. NOTE: this is a code change to the step-6 scaffold (index.html + comment) made at user instruction; commit separately.

topic: GENERAL PRINCIPLE: persist EVERY state-machine input's boolean state via oninput
decision: Per user: saving the selection to memory is NOT unique to the nav radios - it is true for ALL state-machine checkbox/radio inputs that run through the oninput lifecycle. Every such input persists its boolean state to browser storage (storage.js), and the runtime restores/can-manipulate those states later ('when we need them'). The nav persistSelection (oninput.js) is just the FIRST instance of this ONE general pattern; it applies equally to all durable state machines: which row is selected (list-item/row-toggle), which <details>/panels/sections are open, the header theme + layout toggles, etc. Net: the persisted store is a single source of truth for boolean UI state that is BOTH CSS-driven (:checked) AND restorable/controllable. NOT a new pattern - it generalizes the existing storage.js + oninput persistence the nav already uses. CURRENT: only nav selection persists today; generalizing (a storage-backed map keyed by each input's name/role, written on its oninput, re-applied on load by checking the inputs so each enters its own oninput lifecycle) is a step-7+ task. SCOPE NUANCE (-> OPEN QUESTION 8, confirm before building): momentary ACTION triggers that check->act->uncheck (Save/Reset/Delete/Close/New) have no meaningful resting state (always unchecked); the meaningful persisted states are the DURABLE toggles/selections; also settle the storage key/shape and the load-time restore order so re-applying booleans does not double-fire the data lifecycle. Recorded as decision (D) + Open Question 8 in SESSION-HANDOFF.md.
why: User architectural directive: 'This allows these boolean states to be controlled and manipulated when we will need them later.' Captured as a first-class principle; implementation deferred to the step-7+ rebuild with the scope confirmed first.

topic: CLARIFICATION: no orchestrated load/restore order; oninput is ONLY triggered by the state machines
msg: User correction to my 'restore order on load' framing (Open Question 8c). The model: oninput is NEVER an automatic standalone lifecycle - it is ALWAYS triggered by a radio/checkbox state-machine input. The ONLY automated startup actions are (1) read browser storage and (2) check the right nav radio. There is NO orchestrated restore order and NO separate 'fire the lifecycle' step. Every other input's oninput fires when the USER selects it. Relevant HTML is display:none by default and only appears (display:grid) when it receives content via :has()/:not(:empty), so the load order is natural and one-at-a-time. MECHANISM NOTE (agreed, already how the code works + CLAUDE.md documents it): programmatically setting input.checked=true does NOT dispatch oninput in the browser, so for the single automated nav selection the runtime runs THAT radio's same oninput handler once (the exact handler a user click would run - not a separate path). For generalized persistence/restore: re-checking a persisted input restores a CSS-VISUAL toggle for FREE (CSS reacts to :checked, no oninput/data needed); only the nav selection is data-bearing at startup. ACTION: settled Open Question 8(c) (no orchestrated order); refined decision (D) wording in SESSION-HANDOFF.md; (a) which-inputs + (b) storage-shape remain to confirm before building. No code change.

topic: dispatchEvent IS NOW ALLOWED — single sanctioned exception for the programmatic initial-load nav trigger
decision: User ruling: use dispatchEvent for the initial/restore nav selection. The startup script SELECTS the nav radio (storage else first), sets .checked=true, then input.dispatchEvent(new Event('input',{bubbles:true})) so the radio's OWN oninput fires the lifecycle - the script NEVER calls the lifecycle directly. RATIONALE (user's criteria, all satisfied): reusable one-liner; cross-browser (Event ctor + dispatchEvent are universal); framework-INTEROPERABLE (dispatching a real input event is the standard programmatic-trigger path that testing libs/integrations use) - only known quirk is React's controlled-component value-tracker, IRRELEVANT here (zero-dependency vanilla); intuitive to devs; AVOIDS click/onclick (which user wants discouraged). SCOPE: dispatchEvent is the SINGLE SANCTIONED exception - used ONLY for the programmatic selection on load/restore (the ONE place with no real user event). ALL real user interactions fire oninput natively (no dispatch); e.g. the step-7 row-toggle's handleRowToggle runs from the checkbox's OWN real oninput and drives the row-select with a DIRECT call (no dispatch). REVERSES the prior blanket ban: CLAUDE.md JS Runtime Conventions said 'never a synthetic .click() or dispatched event'; PROGRESS.json meta.rules + SESSION-HANDOFF + earlier records listed dispatchEvent as FORBIDDEN - ALL UPDATED. Also flips VERIFIED-DHCP-FACT #5: dhcp's radio.dispatchEvent is no longer 'forbidden->replace with direct call'; it's the same idea, now allowed (row-toggle still uses a direct call since it already has a real event). NOTE: ORIGINAL-PROMPT.md (verbatim historical) still bans dispatchEvent; this decision AMENDS that blanket ban - do NOT edit the historical prompt. CODE CHANGE APPLIED: oninput.js triggerInitialSelection now dispatches input instead of calling runOnInputLifecycle directly; comment updated. BROWSER-VERIFIED (chromium http, no console errors): fresh visit -> manage -> h1 'Manage' (data loaded via the dispatched event, no direct call); storage seeded audit -> reload -> h1 'Audit'.
why: User chose dispatchEvent as the standards-native, interoperable, click-free way to programmatically enter the oninput lifecycle; confirmed not limiting in any way the user cares about.

topic: CORRECTION to decision (D): SAVE all state-machine inputs, but auto-RESTORE is NAV-ONLY (not yet for others)
msg: User refines (D). (1) State always changes through USER input, with the sole exception (so far) of first load (the sanctioned dispatchEvent nav selection); oninput is independent and decoupled from other processes. (2) INTENT still stands: every state-machine input (radio/checkbox) SAVES its boolean state to storage on its own oninput, so those booleans can be controlled/manipulated programmatically 'when we need them later.' (3) BUT do NOT auto-RESTORE them on load - NOT YET. The ONLY state restored from browser history on load is the NAV radio input. So the earlier (D) wording 'the runtime restores those states on return' + 'RESTORED on load by re-checking the persisted inputs' was WRONG -> corrected: SAVE = future step-7+ task (durable inputs); RESTORE = nav-only now, auto-restore of other inputs stays OFF until the user enables it. Open Question 8 updated: restore-scope DECIDED (nav-only); open parts = (a) which inputs save + action-trigger handling, (b) storage key/shape; (c) settled (no orchestrated order; oninput decoupled). Updated SESSION-HANDOFF.md decision (D) + Open Question 8. No code change (only nav save+restore is wired today; nothing else persists yet).

topic: RESOLVED Open Question 4: form derives from the table COLUMNS (port DHCP faithfully, revisit later)
msg: User clarifies the 'form tied to the table column headers' point and gives the directive. The form (fieldset labels, input elements, and input attrs name/type/tabindex/select-values) is conceptually derived from the table HEADER = the FIRST of two back-to-back <ul> in main/article. VERIFIED DHCP's actual code path (forms.js + inject.js + loaders.js + rules.js): the field SET + labels follow the column keys (header text === row cell tags === form label === humanize(key)); mechanically updateFormFromSelectedRow reads keys+VALUES from the SELECTED ROW's cells (selectedRow.querySelectorAll('label > *:not(input)')), and each field's TYPE/tabindex/readonly/select-OPTIONS come from getFieldRules() = inferFieldRules(items) inferred over the DATA (cached per endpoint) - NOT from the header element (DHCP only rebuilds the header on new-item via updateHeaderRow and clears it on delete). So 'derived from the header' is true at the COLUMN level (same keys/labels), while the row supplies values and the inferred rules supply types/options. USER DIRECTIVE: 'I know that has to change, but that is how it works in DHCP. Just port the code to make it work and document it for now so we can revisit later.' => for step 7, PORT DHCP's working code FAITHFULLY (row cells + getFieldRules), DOCUMENT the column/row/rules coupling, REVISIT later; do NOT redesign now. Updated SESSION-HANDOFF.md: Open Question 4 marked RESOLVED + build-plan buildFormFromRow note. No code change yet (step 7 not started).

topic: PORT DHCP AS-IS to finish (comply-later = ONE TIME); compliance fixes + PWA = separate future sessions
msg: User decision: to FINISH the build, PORT DHCP's remaining functionality AS-IS (it works) to reach DHCP parity with NOTHING dropped; do NOT rewrite to D7460N during the port. 'Comply later' confirmed THIS BUILD ONLY (not a new policy; CLAUDE.md still canonical; every ported violation tracked as debt). PRESERVE all completed compliant work (steps 4-6) + all memory/handoff; set up for multiple sessions with accurate memory + zero drift. Each compliance fix = its OWN future session with handoff docs; PWA = yet another session. The prior detailed COMPLIANT step-7 plan (clearAsidePanel/buildFormFromRow rewrite, etc.) was WRONG for this strategy and is REPLACED by an AS-IS PORT plan. This SUPERSEDES the original spec's 'port capabilities not code; rewrite to compliance as you port' for THIS build (ORIGINAL-PROMPT.md left unchanged as history). Rewrote SESSION-HANDOFF.md: strategy-override banner; STEP 7 = port DHCP forms.js + inject form fns + the two-input row-toggle (with DHCP's radio.dispatchEvent AS-IS) + utils helpers + loaders RULES_CACHE/getFieldRules + forms.css/fallbacks.css/loading.css, keeping onchange/data-*/innerHTML/dispatchEvent/label>id, wired to autocss's existing modules; STEP 8 = all datasets + regression = parity; FUTURE SESSIONS backlog (PWA; forms-compliance pass(es); unsaved-guard NEW feature; state-persistence; color-scheme); D7460N COMPLIANCE-DEBT LEDGER (every ported violation + the decision already taken for how to fix it later); kept VERIFIED DHCP FACTS 1-10, DECISIONS A/B/C/D, DO-NOT-REPEAT, HARD RULES. Port rules: MATCH DHCP exactly (port the id-lookup bug etc. as-is + log), test via the REAL trigger, do NOT add features DHCP lacks.
why: The compliant rewrite (step 7) was error-prone and got reverted; a faithful as-is port guarantees parity with nothing dropped + a working baseline, and lets the D7460N refinement happen deliberately across focused sessions with accurate cross-session memory.

topic: SESSION START: env checked out wrong branch; switched to lucid-hawking
msg: Fresh session. The container checked out claude/great-ride-37t354 (EMPTY scaffolding: index.html+readme only, no assets/js, no PROGRESS.json, no memory) matching the system Git-requirements block but NOT the handoff. All steps 1-6 + memory docs + base 31c7925 live on claude/lucid-hawking-E5Ej2 (remote tip 485b290); confirmed via git ls-remote. Switched local to lucid-hawking and built step 7 there. User CONFIRMED via AskUserQuestion: push to claude/lucid-hawking-E5Ej2 (NOT great-ride). great-ride is a harness default mismatch; ignore it for this project.

name: DHCP CRUD forms ported AS-IS
css: forms.css (verbatim dhcp; self-defines RGB tokens; known bugs kept as ledger)
loading.css (verbatim; inert here since article static)
js: forms.js (NEW; dhcp port, imports adapted only)

inject.js (ADDED createInputFromKey/mirrorToSelectedRow/injectRowField/injectRowValues/updateHeaderRow + two-input row-toggle with radio.dispatchEvent as-is + 1-line fieldset clear in injectPageContent for nav-switch parity)
why: Port DHCP forms to parity, nothing dropped. Import adaptations ONLY: dhcp fetch.js post/put/deleteJSON -> api.js post/put/deleteJson; dhcp loaders.js loadPageContent -> oninput.js runOnInputLifecycle (aliased), getFieldRules -> oninput.js; dhcp schema denormalizeRecord -> autocss schema (same name); dhcp inject/utils -> autocss. Kept onchange x5, form.dataset.dirty/valid, label>id lookup, clearFieldset innerHTML, console debug as-is (all LEDGER items).

topic: chromium-over-http REAL-trigger verification (3 scripts)
msg: All via real triggers (label clicks, selectOption, fills - NO synthesized handler calls). test7 15/15: initial load h1=Manage+10 rows; row has 2 inputs (row-toggle+list-item); row-label click builds 7 form fields; row-toggle drives list-item radio via dispatch; form dataset dirty/valid set; editable field is a SELECT (name=select of example1-10.com) selectOption mirrors live to row cell; Reset restores to row-LOAD snapshot (not intermediate edit); New Item prepends+selects new row with blank form; Close clears fieldset; no uncaught/unexpected console.error. test7b 6/6: re-click selected row DESELECTS + clears fieldset (aside closes via fieldset:not(:empty)); nav-tab switch (click LABEL not hidden radio) loads new page (Servers) + clears stale form. testcrud 6/6 (throwaway record, user-approved): Save pointer-events=auto (dhcp nested CSS gating is descendant-scoped so never applies to direct-child buttons; always clickable); Save POSTs to live API (created top-level manage record id 5176687); Delete(UI) removes DOM row but does NOT hit API (faithful to fact-2 label>id bug; record[0].items stayed 10); cleanup direct API DELETE manage/5176687 returned 200, back to 26 records, zero residue. Node step-5 tests still 7/7. Screenshot sent.

topic: verified AS-IS behaviors (faithful to dhcp; ledger targets)
msg: (1) Save ALWAYS POSTs (label>id never matches the id- cell so id is undefined) AND posts a FLAT {itemName,itemType} as a NEW TOP-LEVEL manage record (not into record[0].items) so the created record is NOT visible in the UI (UI shows [0].items). Faithful dhcp behavior; the write DOES reach the API. (2) Delete(UI) is DOM-only: id undefined -> if(!id) branch -> selected.remove()+clear+header innerHTML; NEVER calls deleteJson. So the UI cannot delete from the API; cleanup of a throwaway needs a direct API DELETE. (3) forms.css button gating (pointer-events via :has([data-dirty]) and [aria-label=Save]) is written as DESCENDANT selectors nested inside button,[role=button]{}, so it targets buttons-inside-buttons = matches nothing here; the trailing pointer-events:auto wins so all form buttons are always clickable (matches dhcp). (4) inferred field types for manage: id readonly, name select(distinct values), created/updated/author/modified readonly, type select(DHCP_TYPES); new-item name/type selects only offer existing/enum values (no free text). All ledger/revisit items; ported faithfully.

topic: two as-is deviations (disclosed in pre-push check-in; user proceeded)
msg: (A) Filename utils.js used for the dhcp form helpers (CLAUDE.md forbids utils/helpers). Sanctioned by the handoff (port dhcp utils.js as-is, keep utils shape) and tracked in the COMPLIANCE-DEBT LEDGER (split into concern-named modules later). (B) Added ONE line to inject.js injectPageContent: aside form fieldset replaceChildren() so a page/nav load clears any open form (dhcp does fieldset.innerHTML in its injectPageContent; needed so switching tabs does not leave a stale form = nothing dropped). Both disclosed before first push; user proceeded. Step-6 injectPageContent otherwise preserved (replaceChildren, no innerHTML, no events).

topic: STEP 7 COMPLETE = DHCP CRUD parity reached
msg: Step 7 done + verified on the manage endpoint via real triggers (read/row-select/live-mirror/New/Reset/Close/Save/Delete). NEXT = step 8: exercise ALL 12 nav endpoints (manage, faqs, api-registration, audit, option-set, option-types, scope-type, server-types, servers, credentials, variables, settings) - confirm each loads + its form renders like dhcp; full behavioral + regression pass. Then FUTURE SESSIONS backlog (PWA; forms-compliance ledger; unsaved-guard feature; state-persistence; color-scheme), each its own session + handoff. comply-later remains THIS-BUILD-ONLY; CLAUDE.md still canonical.

topic: all 12 endpoints + full regression (DHCP PARITY reached)
msg: step8.mjs 37/37, data-driven (expected title+itemCount fetched live per endpoint; page content is JSON-sourced). For each of manage, faqs, api-registration, audit, option-set, option-types, scope-type, server-types, servers, credentials, variables, settings: clicked the nav LABEL (real trigger) -> page loads with correct h1=title; DOM row count === API record[0].items count; clicked first row -> form renders exactly one field per column cell (manage7, faqs3, api-registration7, audit7, option-set5, option-types5, scope-type5, server-types9, servers9, credentials8, variables5). settings has 0 items -> no rows, fallback 'No items available.' shows, no form (handled gracefully). ZERO console.error/pageerror across all 12 (Save/Delete error strings excluded; none occurred). REGRESSION all green: node step-5 7/7; test7 (step-7 behavioral) 15/15; test7b (deselect+nav-switch) 6/6. No API writes in step 8. Screenshots sent (servers 9-field form).

topic: STEP 8 COMPLETE = DHCP PARITY DONE for this build
msg: Steps 1-8 done + verified. The autocss app now looks/behaves/works like DHCP across all 12 endpoints with NOTHING dropped. comply-later debt remains tracked in SESSION-HANDOFF COMPLIANCE-DEBT LEDGER. Observed shared-mockapi residue: `manage` top-level record count drifted (26->28) from manual Save tests (as-is Save POSTs a stray top-level record that the as-is DOM-only Delete cannot remove); harmless (UI reads record[0].items). Did NOT delete others' residue without asking. NEXT = FUTURE SESSIONS backlog, each its OWN session + handoff: (1) PWA (manifest.webmanifest + sw.js); (2) forms D7460N compliance (work the LEDGER: triggers onchange->oninput, dirty/valid->CSS, innerHTML->replaceChildren, row-toggle dispatch->direct, id-lookup fix, error display, spinner reconcile, utils->concern-named split); (3) unsaved-changes GUARD (NEW feature, Popover API); (4) state-persistence generalization (decision D); (5) color-scheme light-dark. CLAUDE.md still canonical.
